speakers.Maria: Sam's mother, caring and sometimes overbearing, formal communication style
speakers.Sam: College student (Davis), applying to clinical psych PhD programs, pottery hobby

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Hi sweetie, how's your day going?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: hey mom

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: its fine

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Just fine? What's going on?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: nothing lol just tired

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Are you getting enough sleep? You need at least 8 hours.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: mom

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm just saying! Your father never slept enough in college either.
messages.notes: Casual fact: father's college sleep habits

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok lol

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: How are your classes? You never tell me anything.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: they're good. taking 5 this semester which is a lot

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Five?! That's too many. Which ones?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: intro to psych, calc 2, english comp, bio, and a film studies elective

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Film studies sounds fun at least.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah its my fav honestly. the professor is incredible

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What's the professor's name?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: Dr. Okafor. she did her PhD at NYU on documentary filmmaking

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh wonderful. Are you thinking of minoring in film maybe?
messages.notes: Introduces potential academic path: film minor

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: idk maybe. haven't thought about it

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: You should! You've always loved movies.
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam has always loved movies

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Anyway, I wanted to ask about Thanksgiving. Are you coming home?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah I'm taking the train home Wednesday night

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh good! Aunt Rosa and Uncle Miguel are coming too.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: nice

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm making the green chile tamales you love.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: omg yesss 🙌

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: u always know the way to my heart lol
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam's emotional connection to food

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha! I do know. Oh before I forget — have you started your grad school applications?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ugh not yet mom. deadline isn't til January

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: January is closer than you think.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I knowww. I'll start soon I promise

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Which programs are you applying to?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: clinical psych programs mostly. Berkeley, UCLA, UCSF

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Those are excellent. Have you asked Professor Chen about your recommendation letter?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah she agreed to write one. just need to send her my personal statement first

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: When does she need it by?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: she said December 1st ideally

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK that's very soon! Love you sweetie. Call me Sunday?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: love u too ❤️ yeah I'll try

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Sam, can you call me when you get a chance?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: whats up? in class rn can u text
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam is in class, prefers text

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK. I went to the doctor today.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh is everything ok??

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Well, they found something in my bloodwork that they want to check further.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: wait what do u mean

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: My glucose levels were high. The doctor said I might have prediabetes.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh no mom

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Don't panic, it's not confirmed yet. I have to go back for more tests next week.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok but like are u feeling ok??

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I feel fine honestly. I had no symptoms at all. It was just the routine bloodwork.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok good

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: The doctor said if it IS prediabetes, I'll need to change my diet and exercise more.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok well thats manageable right?

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes, absolutely. It's very common. I just wanted you to know.
messages.notes: Casual medical fact: prediabetes is very common

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: thx for telling me mom

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Of course. I didn't want to worry you but I also don't want to keep secrets.
messages.notes: Emotional: torn about sharing health news

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: no I'm glad u told me. when are the follow up tests?
messages.notes: Question provides context for follow-up test timeline

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Next Thursday the 14th.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok keep me posted pls

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I will. Your father is taking me.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: good. tell dad I said hi

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I will. How are you doing with everything? School OK?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah its fine. I started working on my personal statement actually

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: figured I should stop procrastinating lol
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam admits to procrastination pattern

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh that's wonderful! What are you writing about?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: my experience volunteering at the youth crisis center last summer. and how it made me want to do clinical psych

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: That's perfect. That was such meaningful work you did there.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah it really changed my perspective on a lot of things honestly

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: How long does the statement need to be?
messages.notes: Question establishes statement length requirement context

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: like 500-750 words. its not super long but I want it to be good

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Do you want me to read it when you're done? Your father could too — he wrote his own for med school.
messages.notes: Context: dad went to med school

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah that would be great actually
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam agrees to have parents review statement

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK just send it whenever. No pressure.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok cool

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh and Sam?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah?

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm really proud of you. The applications, the volunteering, all of it.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: 🥺 thx mom
messages.notes: Emotional reaction with 🥺 emoji shows vulnerability

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK get back to class. Love you.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: love u ❤️

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh wait — one more thing. Are you eating enough? You looked thin on FaceTime last week.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: MOM

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK OK going now 😂

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: bye lol

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Sam are you still coming home Wednesday night?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah getting the 6pm train from Davis

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK good. Dad will pick you up at the station.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: cool thx

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I have so much cooking to do this week. The tamales alone take an entire day.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol I believe it

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Aunt Rosa called today. She's bringing her tres leches cake.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh yessss

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I know, it's so good. Uncle Miguel is driving them from Fresno.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: wait they're driving? that's like 3 hours

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes but Miguel hates flying. You know how he is.

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh and your cousin Sofia is coming too! She just got back from her semester in Barcelona.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh nice I haven't seen Sofia in forever
messages.notes: Temporal: Sam hasn't seen Sofia 'in forever'

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I think since the reunion two years ago in Monterey.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah sounds right

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: She apparently learned to make paella while she was in Spain and wants to cook for us.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh that's cool

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: By the way, I got my test results back.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: wait already?? what did they say

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: It IS prediabetes. But the doctor said it's very manageable with diet and exercise.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh no mom. are you ok?

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes honey I'm fine. Really. The doctor put me on a meal plan and I need to walk 30 minutes a day.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok well thats not too bad

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: No it's not. Your father has been very supportive. He's walking with me every morning now.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: aw thats sweet

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes well he could use the exercise too 😂

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lmao

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: The main thing I need to cut down on is sugar and refined carbs.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: wait but what about the tamales lol
messages.notes: Casual callback: tamale plan vs diet

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha! I can still have SOME tamales. Just not ten like last year.
messages.notes: Casual fact: ate ten tamales last year

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol fair

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Anyway, I also wanted to talk about the family reunion.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: wait theres a reunion?

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes! Abuela's 80th birthday is in March. We're doing a big family gathering.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh wow 80!! where is it gonna be

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: We're still deciding. Either at Tía Carmen's house in San Jose or we rent a hall.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: either sounds good. I can prob come if its a weekend

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: It will be Saturday March 15th most likely.

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: We're expecting about 40-50 people from all over.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh wow big party. ok lmk when its confirmed and I'll block it off

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yo mom that was the best thanksgiving

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: It really was! I'm so glad everyone could make it.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: sofias paella was actually so good

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: It was! She's become quite the cook in Spain.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: also aunt rosas tres leches omg

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha I know. She gave me the recipe actually.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh nice. hey so I wanted to tell u something

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What's up?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: so my friend Jake has been doing pottery at this studio near campus
messages.notes: Casual fact: friend Jake, pottery connection

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: and he took me to a class last week and I actually loved it??

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Really? That's wonderful! What did you make?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol just a really lopsided bowl. it was terrible but so fun

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh I'd love to see it!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I'll send u a pic when it comes out of the kiln
messages.notes: Temporal: waiting for kiln to finish

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: but yeah I signed up for the 6-week beginner series. it's every Saturday morning

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh that's exciting. How much does it cost?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: $180 for the series. includes all the materials and kiln time

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: That's very reasonable. Do you need help paying for it?
messages.notes: Context: Maria offers financial help, establishes support pattern

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: nah I got it from my work study money. thanks tho
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam has work-study income

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK just let me know. I think it's wonderful that you're trying something creative.
messages.notes: Maria's opinion: creative pursuits are wonderful

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah idk theres something really calming about it. like u just focus on the clay and everything else disappears

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: You deserve that. With five classes and applications, you need an outlet.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah exactly

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh, did you send Professor Chen your personal statement yet?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: YES I sent it last night actually. she said she'd look at it this week

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh wonderful! How do you feel about it?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: pretty good honestly. it felt really personal to write about the crisis center stuff
messages.notes: Emotional: personal connection to statement content

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm sure it's beautiful. You've always been good with words.
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam has always been good with words

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol ok mom

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What! It's true!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: haha ok ok. thanks ❤️

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: How's everything else? Are you eating properly?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yes mom I eat like 3 meals a day I promise
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam eats 3 meals a day

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK good. Love you. Have a good rest of your week.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: mom I'm freaking out

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What happened?! Are you OK?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah sorry I'm fine. its just the applications
messages.notes: Context clarification: stressed about applications specifically

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK you scared me. What about the applications?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: berkeley's app is due december 15th and I still haven't finished my research proposal
messages.notes: Deadline fact: Berkeley Dec 15 + incomplete proposal

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK that's two weeks away. That's still time.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah but I also have finals coming up AND a bio paper due friday

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Take a deep breath. Make a list. Prioritize.
messages.notes: Maria's advice philosophy: lists and prioritization

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I know I know. im just overwhelmed

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Can you talk to any of your professors about extensions?
messages.notes: Suggests professor extensions, provides strategic context

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: maybe. Dr. Okafor is pretty flexible usually

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ask her. The worst she can say is no.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: true

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: also the research proposal is hard bc I want to write about adolescent trauma interventions but idk if thats too broad

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Have you talked to Professor Chen about it? She's your advisor right?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah shes my academic advisor. I have a meeting with her tomorrow actually

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Perfect. She can help you narrow it down.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah hopefully. how r u btw. hows the prediabetes stuff

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Good actually! I've been following the meal plan for two weeks now and I already feel better.

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: My blood sugar is trending down according to the glucose monitor.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh thats great mom!!

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes and your father and I walk every morning now. We do about 2 miles along the river trail.
messages.notes: Exercise routine detail: 2 miles, river trail

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: aw thats cute. couple goals

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha! He complains the whole time but he shows up.
messages.notes: Casual fact: dad complains but walks with Maria daily

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lmao classic dad

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: My next checkup is in January. The doctor wants to see if the numbers improve enough to avoid medication.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: fingers crossed 🤞

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Thank you sweetie. Oh, how's pottery going?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: SO GOOD actually

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I made a mug this week that actually looks like a real mug lol

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: and the instructor said I have a natural feel for the wheel

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh that's wonderful! Send me a photo.
messages.notes: Request provides context: Maria wants to see pottery progress

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I will when its glazed. I'm doing this blue-green glaze that Jake recommended

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I can't wait to see it. Maybe you can make me something for Christmas?
messages.notes: Temporal: Christmas gift suggestion establishes timeline

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: haha maybe!! I was actually thinking about that

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I would treasure anything you make. OK go study. Love you.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok ok. love u too mom. and seriously glad about the health stuff ❤️

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Thank you baby. You focus on those apps!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I will I will byeee

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Bye! 💕

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: MOM

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I DID IT

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What?! Tell me!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I submitted the Berkeley application

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OH SAM!! 🎉🎉

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm so proud of you!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol thanks it feels so good to have it done

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: How do you feel about it? The research proposal and everything?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: actually really good. Professor Chen helped me narrow it down to adolescent PTSD interventions specifically

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh that's so focused and meaningful.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah and my personal statement ties into it perfectly with the crisis center experience

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: When will you hear back?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: they said decisions come out in March

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: March! That's a long wait.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I know 😩 but at least the hard part is done
messages.notes: Temporal: hard part is done, relief marker

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: UCLA is due January 5th and UCSF is January 15th so I still have two more

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: But you can mostly reuse material right?
messages.notes: Context: can reuse material across applications

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah basically. each school has slightly different essay prompts tho

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Well you'll get through it. The hard part was starting.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: true. oh btw I finished my pottery series!!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I made you a bowl and a little planter for christmas 🎄

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Sam!! Oh my heart. I can't wait.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol they're not perfect but I tried

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: They'll be perfect because you made them.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok ur gonna make me cry in the library rn

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha! Sorry. When are you coming home for Christmas?
messages.notes: Temporal: when coming home for Christmas

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: dec 20th. driving down with Jake actually hes going to Sacramento too
messages.notes: Travel plan: Dec 20, driving with Jake to Sacramento

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Jake the pottery friend?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah he lives in Sac. well his parents do

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh how convenient! Is he a good driver? 😂

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol yes mom he has a prius

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK good. I'm so excited to see you. The house is already decorated.
messages.notes: Casual fact: house is already decorated for Christmas

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: can't wait ❤️

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Love you sweetie. Go celebrate tonight! You deserve it.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: haha we're getting boba later. love u too!

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Did you get home safely?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah jake just dropped me off. back in davis

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Good. It was so wonderful having you home.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: it was really nice. thanks for everything mom

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm using the bowl you made me every morning for my oatmeal. I love it.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: omg really?? that makes me so happy
messages.notes: Emotional: joy about gift being used

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes! And the planter is on the kitchen windowsill with the basil plant.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: perf 🌿

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Your aunt Rosa said she wants to commission you to make her some serving bowls.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lmao I'm not that good yet

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: She was serious! She'll pay you.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: haha ok tell her I'll think about it

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I will. How are the other applications coming?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: UCLA is basically done. submitting tomorrow

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh wonderful! And UCSF?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: still working on that one. the essay prompt is weird. they want you to describe a clinical scenario

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: That sounds challenging. Can you use your crisis center experience?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah thats what im doing. writing about this one situation where I helped a teenager who was having a panic attack

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh Sam. That must have been so difficult.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: it was. but also like the most meaningful thing I've ever done honestly
messages.notes: Emotional: deep meaning from volunteer work

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: she ended up coming back the next week and asked for me specifically

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: See? You have a gift for this.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: thanks mom 🥺

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Have you thought about what happens if you get into more than one program?
messages.notes: Context: what if accepted to multiple programs

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah I mean Berkeley is my top choice by far. the clinical psych program there is ranked like top 5 nationally
messages.notes: Preference: Berkeley is top choice + ranking

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: And it's close enough that you could come home on weekends sometimes.
messages.notes: Casual fact: Berkeley is close enough for weekend visits

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: true. plus I already know the Bay Area from visiting Sofia
messages.notes: Casual fact: visited Bay Area via cousin

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Exactly. Oh speaking of, we finalized the reunion details.

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: It's going to be at Tía Carmen's house in San Jose. March 15th.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh nice. her house is huge right?
messages.notes: Context about venue: Carmen's house is huge

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes perfect for a big gathering. We're expecting about 45 family members.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: wow thats a lot of primos

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha! Yes it is. Abuela is so excited. She keeps calling me to add people to the list.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol classic abuela

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: She wants you to bring your pottery to show everyone.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: omg no thats embarrassing

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: She's proud of you! We all are.
messages.notes: Emotional: family pride expressed by Maria

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok ok fine. maybe I'll bring one piece lol

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Good. OK I'll let you go. Finish that UCSF application! Love you.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: on it rn!! love u ❤️

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: all three apps submitted 🎉🎉🎉
messages.notes: Life event: all grad apps done

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: YESSS!! Berkeley, UCLA, and UCSF?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yep all three. I'm freeeee

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm so proud of you Sam. That was so much work.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: it really was lol. like the last month has been insane
messages.notes: Temporal: 'the last month has been insane'

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: When do you hear back from UCLA and UCSF?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: UCLA is late February. UCSF is mid-March

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK so Berkeley is March, UCLA is February, UCSF is March. Got it.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah its gonna be a stressful couple months lol

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Just try to enjoy the rest of your semester. You've earned it.
messages.notes: Maria's advice: enjoy the semester, you've earned it

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah I plan to. actually I wanted to tell u something about pottery

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh what?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: so the studio offered me a spot in their intermediate wheel-throwing class

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh how exciting! When does it start?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: next week. its 8 weeks and we learn glazing techniques and larger forms
messages.notes: Course details: 8 weeks, glazing + large forms

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: That sounds wonderful. More expensive though?
messages.notes: Context question: cost of intermediate course

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah $250 but they gave me a 15% discount cuz I finished the beginner series

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Do you want me to pay for it? Consider it a late Christmas gift.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: mom u dont have to

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I want to. You're talented and I want to support that.
messages.notes: Maria's values: supporting talent and creativity

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok fine 😭 thank you

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: You're welcome! Just keep making me pottery.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: deal lol

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh also Jake and I might start selling stuff at the Davis farmers market
messages.notes: Business plan: farmers market pottery sales

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What?! Really?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah the instructor connected us with the market organizer. you just need to apply for a booth

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh Sam that would be amazing. What would you sell?
messages.notes: Context question about farmers market products

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: mugs and bowls mostly. maybe some planters. we'd split a booth, like $50 each per weekend

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: That's very entrepreneurial of you.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol idk about that but it could be fun

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I think it's wonderful. Your father will be thrilled.
messages.notes: Casual fact: Maria thinks father will be thrilled about market

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: haha yeah probably. ok I gotta go to class

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK sweetie. Oh wait — how's my health update?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh yeah!! how was the January checkup?

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Great news actually! My glucose levels dropped significantly. Doctor said no medication needed for now.
messages.notes: Life event: health improvement, no meds

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: MOM THATS AMAZING

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yes! The walking and diet changes are working. Just need to keep it up.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: im so happy. seriously. like so relieved

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Me too sweetie. OK go to class for real. Love you!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: love u!! ❤️❤️

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Good morning sweetie! How are you?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: morning mom

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I'm ok. kinda anxious honestly

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: About the applications?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah UCLA decisions should be coming out soon. like any day now

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh how exciting. And nerve-wracking I'm sure.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah lol I check my email like every 5 minutes
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam checks email every 5 minutes

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I remember when your father was waiting for his med school acceptance. He was a wreck.
messages.notes: Casual fact: dad's med school anxiety

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol some things are genetic I guess
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam compares own anxiety to father's

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha! True. Listen, whatever happens, I'm proud of you no matter what.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: thx mom ❤️

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: So the reunion is in less than a month. March 15th at Tía Carmen's.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah I have it on my calendar
messages.notes: Context: Sam has reunion on calendar

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Good. I'm coordinating the food with Rosa and Carmen. We need to figure out seating too.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol u r such a planner mom
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam characterizes Maria as a planner

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Someone has to be! Abuela keeps trying to invite more people. We're up to 50 now.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lmao 50?? is there even room

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Carmen's backyard is huge. Plus we're renting tables and chairs.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok fair. what do u need me to do
messages.notes: Context: Sam asks what they can help with

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Can you help with the music? You always have good taste.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh yeah I can make a playlist

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Perfect. A mix of everything — some old school for Abuela, some modern stuff for you kids.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol 'you kids.' I'm 22 mom
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam is 22 years old

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: You'll always be my kid. Oh, are you bringing anyone? A friend? A... special someone?
messages.notes: Casual fact: Maria asks about 'special someone', reveals curiosity

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: MOM

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What?! I'm just asking!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: no I'm coming solo lol. I'll drive with sofia actually, she said she'd pick me up from the train station

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh that's perfect. You two can catch up on the way.
messages.notes: Context: Sofia can pick Sam up, they'll catch up

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah. hey can I ask u something about abuela

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Of course.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: do u think she'd like a pottery piece from me? like as a birthday gift

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh Sam she would LOVE that. She talks about your pottery all the time.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: wait she does?? I only showed her one piece

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I sent her photos of everything you've made. She shows all her friends.
messages.notes: Casual fact: Maria shares Sam's work with abuela

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: omg mom 😭

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What?! I'm a proud mother!
messages.notes: Casual fact: Maria is 'a proud mother' who shares Sam's work

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok well I'll make her something special. maybe a vase

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: She'll treasure it forever. Oh, one more thing — the farmers market. How's that going?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: oh so we did our first weekend last Saturday! Jake and I split a booth

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: How did it go?!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: we sold 6 mugs and 3 bowls!! made like $180 total

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: That's incredible for your first time!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: right?? people really liked the glazes. this one woman bought 3 mugs as gifts

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm beaming right now. OK I have to go start dinner. Love you so much.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: love u too mom ❤️ I'll let u know the SECOND I hear from UCLA

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: MOM MOM MOM

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: CALL ME RN

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: What?! What happened?! Is everything OK?!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: YES OMG YES

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I GOT INTO UCLA

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: WHAT

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: SAM

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OH MY GOD!! 🎉🎉🎉

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I KNOW I'M LITERALLY CRYING RN

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: I'm crying too!! Oh honey I'm so proud of you!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: the email came like 20 minutes ago. I screamed in the library

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Ha!! Tell me everything. What did the letter say?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: ok so its for the Clinical Psychology PhD program. full admission with funding!!

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: FUNDING?!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah they're offering a full tuition waiver plus a $32,000 annual stipend
messages.notes: Financial package: tuition waiver + stipend amount

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh Sam. Oh my God. That's... that's everything.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I know. I literally cant believe it
messages.notes: Emotional: Sam in disbelief about acceptance

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: its a 5-year program. first 2 years are coursework and the last 3 are research and clinical practice

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: And they're PAYING you to do it. Your father is going to lose his mind.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol tell him!! I tried calling but he didn't pick up
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam tried calling dad, no answer

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: He's in surgery right now. I'll tell him the SECOND he's out.
messages.notes: Casual fact: dad is a surgeon, currently in OR

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Oh honey, have you heard from Berkeley or UCSF yet?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: not yet. berkeley is early march and ucsf is mid-march

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Well you already have UCLA! With funding! Whatever else happens is just a bonus.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: yeah honestly even if I don't get in anywhere else I'm SO happy with UCLA

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: The campus is beautiful. When would you start?
messages.notes: Temporal question: when would Sam start at UCLA

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: fall semester. September

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: That's exciting. You'd be in Los Angeles!

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: I know!! I've always wanted to live in LA honestly
messages.notes: Preference: wants to live in LA

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: We'll need to help you find an apartment. Does the program have housing?

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: theres grad student housing yeah. I need to look into it
messages.notes: Housing option: grad student housing available

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: We'll figure it all out. For now just CELEBRATE.

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lol jake and I are going out tonight for sure

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Good! You deserve it. Oh Sam, wait until Abuela hears at the reunion.
messages.notes: Callback to reunion: sharing news there

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: omg she's gonna lose it

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: She's going to tell EVERYONE. Her grandbaby the doctor. Well, psychologist.
messages.notes: Casual fact: abuela will call Sam 'doctor/psychologist'

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: lmaooo not a doctor yet mom

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: Yet! I'm telling all my friends too. My coworker Diana's daughter just got rejected from 3 programs and I feel bad but also—
messages.notes: Casual fact: Maria's coworker Diana, social comparison

messages.speaker: Sam
messages.content: MOM don't be that person lol
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sam tells Maria not to be braggy

messages.speaker: Maria
messages.content: OK OK I'll be tasteful about it. LOVE YOU SO MUCH. ❤️🎉
